verses.sanskrit: हत्वा तु तम् भीम बलम् विराधम् राक्षसम् वने ।ततः सीताम् परिष्वज्य समाश्वास्य च वीर्यवान् ॥३-५-१॥
verses.text:
After killing that stupendously mighty demon Viradha in forest, then Rama comfortingly embraced Seetha, and spoke to his 
brother Lakshmana who is beaming forth with resplendence. [3-5-1]

verses.sanskrit: अब्रवीत् भ्रातरम् रामो लक्ष्मणम् दीप्त तेजसम् ।कष्टम् वनम् इदम् दुर्गम् न च स्मो वन गोचराः ॥३-५-२॥
verses.text:
"Difficult is this impassable forest, nor we are forest inhabitants either... let's go quickly to that ascetically rich 
sage Sharabhanga..." [3-5-2b, 3a]

verses.sanskrit: अभिगच्छामहे शीघ्रम् शरभङ्गम् तपो धनम् ।आश्रमम् शरभन्गस्य राघवोऽभिजगाम ह ॥३-५-३॥
verses.text:
On his approach at the hermitage of Sage Sharabhanga, a sage with a divine effect and the one with a purified soul, saw 
a great wonder at the nearby of that hermitage of Sharabhanga.[3-5-3b, 4]

verses.sanskrit: तस्य देव प्रभावस्य तपसा भावित आत्मनः ।समीपे शरभंगस्य ददर्श महत् अद्भुतम् ॥३-५-४॥
विभ्राजमानम् वपुषा सूर्य वैश्वानर प्रभम् ।रथ प्रवरम् आरूढम् आकाशे विबुध अनुगम् ॥३-५-५॥
verses.text:
Rama has seen Indra, the lord of three worlds, with a radiant body that has the coupled radiance of Sun and Fire, mounted 
on a great chariot that does not touch the earth, wearing greatly brilliant ornaments and attired in spotless dress, while 
the other divinities are attending, and many more selfsame great-souls are worshipping him. [3-5-5, 6, 7a]

verses.sanskrit: असंस्पृशन्तम् वसुधाम् ददर्श विबुध ईश्वरम् ।संप्रभ आभरणम् देवम् विरजो अंबर धारिणम् ॥३-५-६॥
तत् विधैः एव बहुभिर् पूज्यमानम् महात्मभिः ।हरितैः वाजिभिर् युक्तम् अंतरिक्ष गतम् रथम् ॥३-५-७॥
verses.text:
Rama saw at his very nearby a chariot yoked with green horses that traverses in firmament, which in its splendour is either 
like the noontime sun, or a cluster of silver clouds, or like the silvern sphere of the moon. [3-5-7b, 8]

verses.sanskrit: ददर्श अदूरतः तस्य तरुण आदित्य संनिभम् ।पाण्डुर अभ्र घन प्रख्यम् चन्द्र मण्डल संनिभम् ॥३-५-८॥
अपश्यत् विमलम् छत्रम् चित्र माल्य उपशोभितम् ।चामर व्यजने च अग्र्ये रुक्म दण्डे महाधने ॥३-५-९॥
verses.text:
He saw the stainless parasol of Indra, marvellously garlanded and shining forth, and the exemplary and invaluable royal 
fanning instruments with golden handles, which two best female celestials are handling and fanning at Indra's head sides 
with them. [3-5-9, 10a]

verses.sanskrit: गृहीते वर नारीभ्याम् धूयमाने च मूर्धनि ।गन्धर्व अमर सिद्धाः च बहवः परम ऋषयः ॥३-५-१०॥
verses.text:
And the celestials like gandharva-s, amara-s, siddhaa-s and very many great sages as well are extolling Indra who is abiding 
in firmament. [3-5-10b, 11a]

verses.sanskrit: अन्तरिक्ष गतम् देवम् गीर्भिर् अग्र्याभिर् ऐडियन् ।सह संभाषमाणे तु शरभंगेन वासवे ॥३-५-११॥
verses.text: On seeing Indra speaking there to sage Sharabhanga, Rama spoke to Lakshmana. [3-5-11b, 12a]

verses.sanskrit: दृष्ट्वा शत क्रतुम् तत्र रामो लक्ष्मणम् अब्रवीत् ।रामोऽथ रथम् उद्दिश्य भ्रातुर् दर्शयत अद्भुतम् ॥३-५-१२॥
verses.text:
Rama then on pointing out the chariot expressed his own amazement saying, "Lakshmana see this wonderful chariot that includes 
effulgence and auspiciousness as well, and that abides in firmament, and highly luminous like the Sun... [3-5-12b, 13]

verses.sanskrit: अर्चिष्मन्तम् श्रिया जुष्टम् अद्भुतम् पश्य लक्ष्मण ।प्रतपन्तम् इव आदित्यम् अन्तरिक्ष गतम् रथम् ॥३-५-१३॥
ये हयाः पुरु हूतस्य पुरा शक्रस्य नः श्रुताः ।अन्तरिक्ष गता दिव्याः ते इमे हरयो ध्रुवम् ॥३-५-१४॥
verses.text:
"Of which horses we have earlier heard from our teachers telling that they belong to Indra, the Chief Invitee in Vedic rituals, 
they are these divinely green horses that now abide in the firmament, and definitely these must be Indra's horses... [3-5-14]

verses.sanskrit: इमे च पुरुष व्याघ्र ये तिष्ठन्ति अभितः दिशम् ।शतम् शतम् कुण्डलिनो युवानः खड्ग पाणयः ॥३-५-१५॥
verses.text:
"And oh, manly-tiger Lakshmana, those that are there in hundreds and hundreds around Indra, those youthful ones with their 
ear-ornaments and swords in hands, with wide and broad chests, with club shaped arms, and clad in glimmering red garments, 
all are like tigers, all are unapproachable, all of them are wearing ornamental chains on their chest-place that are akin 
to flaring fires, and in their appearance, Soumitri, they always bear a look of twenty-five-year-olds... [3-5-15, 16, 17]

verses.sanskrit: विस्तीर्ण विपुल उरस्काः परिघायत बाहवः ।शोणांशु वसनाः सर्वे व्याघ्र इव दुरासदाः ॥३-५-१६॥
उरो देशेषु सर्वेषाम् हारा ज्वलन संनिभाः ।रूपम् बिभ्रति सौमित्रे पंच विंशति वार्षिकम् ॥३-५-१७॥
एतद्धि किल देवानाम् वयो भवति नित्यदा ।यथा इमे पुरुष व्याघ्रा दृश्यन्ते प्रिय दर्शनाः ॥३-५-१८॥
verses.text:
"The age factor of gods remains constant at the age that appears for these nice-looking Tigerly-men, so they say... 
for gods are immortals and they will be ever young... [3-5-18]

verses.sanskrit: इह एव सह वैदेह्या मुहूर्तम् तिष्ठ लक्ष्मण ।यावत् जानामि अहम् व्यक्तम् क एष द्युतिमान् रथे ॥३-५-१९॥
verses.text: "Stay for a moment Lakshmana along with Vaidehi, until I know clearly about that resplendent one on that chariot... [3-5-19]

verses.sanskrit: तम् एवम् उक्त्वा सौमित्रिम् इह एव स्थीयताम् इति ।अभिचक्राम काकुत्स्थः शरभंग आश्रमम् प्रति ॥३-५-२०॥
verses.text: On saying to Lakshmana stay there only...' Rama proceeded towards the hermitage of Sage Sharabhanga.. [3-5-20]

verses.sanskrit: ततः समभिगच्छन्तम् प्रेक्ष्य रामम् शची पतिः ।शरभंगम् अनुज्ञाप्य विबुधान् इदम् अब्रवीत् ॥३-५-२१॥
verses.text:
Then, on seeing Rama arriving there, Indra, bade farewell to Sage Sharabhanga and said this to the celestials attending 
him. [3-5-21]

verses.sanskrit: इह उपयाति असौ रामो यावन् माम् न अभिभाषते ।निष्ठाम् नयत तावत् तु ततो मा द्रष्टुम् अर्हति ॥३-५-२२॥
verses.text:
"Here comes that Rama...before he talks to me, you lead him towards his vow, after completing the result of his incarnation, 
then only he is eligible to see me... Let him be a victorious and accomplished one, then I will see him very soon...for 
that will happen very soon... A great deed is to be verily performed by him that is highly impossible for others... [3-5-22, 
23

verses.sanskrit: जितवन्तम् कृतार्थम् हि तदा अहम् अचिराद् इमम् ।कर्म हि अनेन कर्तव्यम् महत् अन्यैः सुदुष्करम् ॥३-५-२३॥
अथ वज्री तम् आमंत्र्य मानयित्वा च तापसम् ।रथेन हय युक्तेन ययौ दिवम् अरिन्दमः ॥३-५-२४॥
verses.text:
Then that Enemy-destroyer Indra adoringly bade farewell to the sage, and travelled to heavens by the chariot with its green 
horses. [3-5-24]

verses.sanskrit: प्रयाते तु सहस्राक्षे राघवः सपरिच्छदः ।अग्नि होत्रम् उपासीनम् शरभंगम् उपागमत् ॥३-५-२५॥
verses.text:
On the transit of that Thousand-eyed Indra, Raghava with his adherents [namely Lakshmana and Seetha,] came nearby the 
Sage Sharabhanga, who is sitting nearby the altar of fire. [3-5-25]

verses.sanskrit: तस्य पादौ च संगृह्य रामः सीता च लक्ष्मणः ।निषेदुः तद् अनुज्ञाता लब्ध वासा निमंत्रिताः ॥३-५-२६॥
verses.text:
Touching the feet of that Sage Sharabhanga Rama along with Seetha and Lakshmana, and duly permitted and invited by that 
Sage Sharabhanga they sat there, as they obtained a shelter. [3-5-26]

verses.sanskrit: ततः शक्र उपयानम् तु पर्यपृच्छत राघवः ।शरभंगः च तत् सर्वम् राघवाय न्यवेदयत् ॥३-५-२७॥
verses.text: Then, Raghava enquired with sage about the arrival of Indra and Sage Sharabhanga narrated all that to him. [3-5-27]

verses.sanskrit: माम् एष वरदो राम ब्रह्म लोकम् निनीषति ।जितम् उग्रेण तपसा दुष्प्रापम् अकृत आत्मभिः ॥३-५-२८॥
verses.text:
"Oh! Rama, that boon-giver Indra wishes to take me to Brahma's abode, which is achieved by me by my arduous ascesis and 
that which is unattainable for them with their souls unconquered... [3-5-28]

verses.sanskrit: अहम् ज्ञात्वा नर व्याघ्र वर्तमानम् अदूरतः ।ब्रह्म लोकम् न गच्छामि त्वाम् अदृष्ट्वा प्रिय अतिथिम् ॥३-५-२९॥
verses.text:
"On knowing that you are course is nearby, oh! Manly-tiger, and without seeing my dear guest I do not wish to go to Brahma's 
abode... [3-5-29]

verses.sanskrit: त्वया अहम् पुरुषव्याघ्र धार्मिकेण महत्मनाअ ।समागम्य गमिष्यामि त्रिदिवम् च अवरम् परम् ॥३-५-३०॥
verses.text:
" Having met with you, a virtuous one and a great soul as you are, oh, manly-tiger, I wish to go through undesirable lesser 
heavens to Brahma's abode, the highest [3-5-30]

verses.sanskrit: अक्षया नर शार्दूल जितालोका मया शुभाः ।ब्राह्म्याः च नाक पृष्ठ्याः च प्रतिगृह्णीष्व मामकान् ॥३-५-३१॥
verses.text:
"Oh! Tigerly-man, non-diminishing are the abodes achieved by me either in Brahma's abode or yonder than paradise...you take 
them all...that are mine achieved by me ascesis..." So said Sage Sharabhanga to Rama. [3-5-31]

verses.sanskrit: एवम् उक्तो नरव्याघ्रः सर्व शास्त्र विशारदः ।ऋषिणा शरभंगेन राघवो वाक्यम् अब्रवीत् ॥३-५-३२॥
verses.text:
Thus said by the Sage Sharabhanga to that Manly-tiger Rama, being the scholar in all the scriptures he spoke this sentence 
to that sage. [3-5-32]

verses.sanskrit: अहम् एव आहरिष्यामि सर्वान् लोकान् महामुने ।आवासम् तु अहम् इच्छामि प्रदिष्टम् इह कानने ॥३-५-३३॥
verses.text:
"I alone can gain all those worlds, oh! Great Saint, I now seek a place to dwell here in this forest as indicated by you... 
[Apparent meaning.] [3-5-33] "I will however take all those worlds of yours, for you have waited for me and dedicated whatever you achieved by your penance 
at my tri-feet, as atithi satkaara , honouring guests - ' leaving 'the doer and doing' attachment, 
and without desire to enjoy the fruits of works, as a karma phala tyaagi . For now, show me 
a place to dwell in this forest like an ordinary human being - [Implicit meaning according to Govindaraja.] "I will win-over all those worlds myself, as Kshatriya-s are not supposed to take any donations; they either have to won 
by them or acquire them for themselves, just show me a place to dwell - [Maheshvara Tirtha.] "Everything merges in me alone, for I alone gave all these to you, and you also dedicated the same in me, without the concept 
of doer and doing - [which again is reiterated in Bhagavad Gita - labhatecha tataH kaamaan mayaiva vihitaan || 7-22 ' All the desires of the adherent like Brahma's abode, immortality etc., are acquired at my grace.' for now show 
me a place to live - [Another concept. tani sloki.]

verses.sanskrit: राघवेण एवम् उक्तः तु शक्र तुल्य बलेन वै ।शरभंगो महाप्राज्ञः पुनर् एव अब्रवीत् वचः ॥३-५-३४॥
verses.text:
Thus said by Raghava, who is like Indra in strength, that supremely intellectual Sage Sharabhanga again spoke this sentence 
this way. [3-5-34]

verses.sanskrit: इह राम महातेजाः सुतीक्ष्णो नम धार्मिकः ।वसति अरण्ये नियतः स ते श्रेयो विधास्यति ॥३-५-३५॥
verses.text:
"Here, oh! Rama, a great resplendent sage named Suteekshna, lives in this forest...a virtuous and a self-disciplined one, 
and he will do something benignant to you... [3-5-35]

verses.sanskrit: सुतीक्ष्णम् अभिगच्छ त्वम् शुचौ देशे तपस्विनम् ।रमणीये वनोद्देशे स ते वासम् विधास्यति ॥३-५-३६॥
verses.text:
"You approach Sage Suteekshna and to you he will arrange housing in his auspicious and dpleasant place woods of hermits... 
[3-5-36]

verses.sanskrit: इमाम् मन्दाकिनीम् राम प्रतिस्रोतम् अनुव्रज ।नदीम् पुष्पोडुप वहाम् ततः तत्र गमिष्यसि ॥३-५-३७॥
verses.text:
" Following opposite to its flow you may follow this River Mandaakini that carries flower-ferries, then you can reach there 
at Sage Suteekshna's hermitage...] [3-5-37]

verses.sanskrit: एष पन्था नरव्याघ्र मुहूर्तम् पश्य तात माम् ।यावत् जहामि गात्राणि जीर्णाम् त्वचम् इव उरगः ॥३-५-३८॥
verses.text:
"This is the only way, oh, manly-tiger, and oh, sire, see me for a moment, till I forsake these body parts of mine, like 
a snake casting its withered moult... [3-5-38]

verses.sanskrit: ततो अग्निम् सु समाधाय हुत्वा च आज्येन मंत्रवित् ।शरभंगो महातेजाः प्रविवेश हुताशनम् ॥३-५-३९॥
verses.text:
Then that hymnist on invoking the sacred fire and offering oblations with clarified butter into it, that radiant sage Sharabhanga 
entered that sacred fire. [3-5-39]

verses.sanskrit: तस्य रोमाणि केशाम् च तदा वह्निः महात्मनः ।जीर्णम् त्वचम् तद् अस्थीनि यत् च मांसम् च शोणितम् ॥३-५-४०॥
verses.text:
The fire burnt down that great soul Sharabhanga from head-hair to body hair and thus his shrunk skin, bones and whatever 
flesh and blood are there, they are also burnt completely. [3-5-40] Those who obtained divine knowledge about this mortal body, and those that cannot practice anuSThaana their daily 
chores, or with any incapability, and those that have left house as holder, and desiccated, could give up their mortal bodies 
into fire, waters or falling from mountain peaks.

verses.sanskrit: स च पावक संकाशः कुमारः समपद्यत ।उत्थाय अग्निचयात् तस्मात् शरभंगो व्यरोचत ॥३-५-४१॥
verses.text:
He that Sage Sharabhanga, then reappeared as an youngster in the semblance of Fire, o\n resurrecting from that altar of 
fire he shone-forth. [3-5-41]

verses.sanskrit: स लोकान् आहिताग्नीनाम् ऋषीणाम् च महात्मनाम् ।देवानाम् च व्यतिक्रम्य ब्रह्म लोकम् व्यरोहत ॥३-५-४२॥
verses.text:
He that Sage Sharabhanga, transcending the ethereal worlds of fire-worshippers, of saints, of great souls, and also of divinities, 
has ascended to Brahma's abode. [3-5-42]

verses.sanskrit: स पुण्य कर्मा भुवने द्विजर्षभःपितामहम् सानुचरम् ददर्श ह ।पितामहः च अपि समीक्ष्य तम् द्विजम्ननन्द सुस्वागतम् इति उवाच ह  ॥३-५-४३॥
verses.text:
He that meritorious and best Brahman Sharabhanga has seen Brahma in Brahma's abode, and Brahma too, on seeing that Brahman 
happily spoke to him... "Welcome..." [3-5-43]
